title: BXP to Present at Upcoming Investor Conferences
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--BXP (NYSE: BXP), the largest publicly traded developer, owner, and manager of premier workplaces in the United States, announced today that Douglas Linde – President and Michael LaBelle – Chief Financial Officer, will participate in and present at the following investor conferences: Barclays Global Financial Services Conference Date: September 11, 2023 Location: New York, NY Presentation: 10:30 AM – 11:10 AM BofA Securities Global Real Estate Conference Date: September.

title: Never Forget: REITs Own Property Run By People
text: REIT stocks have not performed well this year, but the quality of the management team is an important factor in determining the success of a REIT. Examples of REITs with strong management teams include Realty Income, Prologis, Alexandria Real Estate, Rexford Industrial, Mid-America Apartment, and Camden Property Trust. These REITs have experienced positive AFFO growth and have strong balance sheets, making them attractive investment opportunities.

title: REITs That Create Value From The Ground Up
text: As a former commercial real estate developer, I can share insight and experiences from my career in the industry. We emphasize the importance of working with various professionals, such as bankers and contractors, to successfully complete real estate projects. We discuss the value of a CRE background in understanding and analyzing REIT opportunities, highlighting specific REITs as strong investment options.

title: 3 Sorry Dividend Stocks to Sell in August Before It's Too Late
text: Dividend stocks have become investors' favorites in recent months. Tech turbulence and high-interest rates combined to make high-yield stocks an alternative to riskier equities while still beating the yield on fixed-income investments like treasuries.

title: Don't Fall for These 2 Dividend Stocks: Cuts Are Coming
text: Dividends are at the discretion of the boards, which generally don't like to cut them unless they have no choice. Medical Properties Trust is facing material headwinds that have caused management to walk back its language about dividend sustainability.

title: 5 REITs That Will Likely Cut Their Dividends
text: Not all REIT dividends are sustainable. Quite a few will have to cut their dividend in the coming quarters. We highlight 5 of them.

title: Boston Properties: Solid Leasing Improves The Outlook
text: Boston Properties is a high-quality office REIT with a focus on established legacy markets and A-Class buildings. Premier buildings have maintained stable occupancy while non-premier buildings have seen a decline. The company has met leasing targets and raised full-year guidance, but faces headwinds from high interest rates and debt maturities.

title: Boston Properties: Solid Q2 Result Reassures Solid Dividend Yield
text: BXP has reported solid second-quarter results, outperforming market expectations despite economic headwinds. BXP offers an attractive dividend yield of 5.84%, making it a good investment option for risk-averse and retired investors. After comparing the forward P/E ratio of 9.21x with the sector median of 12.89x, I think the company is undervalued.

title: 5 Best Office REITs to Buy Now
text: Real estate investing isn't only for millionaires — retail investors can get a shot at investing in major commercial real estate endeavors by investing in REITs. Specialty REITs help investors refine their investment preferences by capitalizing on tenant returns tied to specific industries.

title: Boston Properties (BXP) Q2 FFO and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Boston Properties' (BXP) Q2 results outperform estimates on better-than-anticipated revenues, aided by healthy leasing activity. It has also raised its 2023 guidance for FFO per share.

title: Boston Properties (BXP) Q2 FFO and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Boston Properties (BXP) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $1.86 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.80 per share. This compares to FFO of $1.94 per share a year ago.

title: BXP Announces Second Quarter 2023 Results; Reports Q2 EPS of $0.66 and FFO Per Share of $1.86
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--BXP (NYSE: BXP), the largest publicly traded developer, owner, and manager of premier workplaces in the United States, reported results today for the second quarter ended June 30, 2023. Financial highlights for the second quarter include: Revenue increased 5.6% to $817.2 million for the quarter ended June 30, 2023, compared to $773.9 million for the quarter ended June 30, 2022. Net income attributable to common shareholders of $104.3 million, or $0.66 per diluted share.
